message.content: Adversaries may exploit Plist File Modification to alter macOS property list files (plist files) for malicious purposes, potentially evading system defenses. Plist files, used by macOS applications to store configuration settings, can be modified to influence system behaviors, such as hiding application execution or enabling persistence. Common exploitation methods include adding malicious paths to plist files, modifying keys to run applications in the background, and inserting environment variables for persistence. For instance, adversaries can use the `plutil` command to change plist file keys, affecting application visibility and behavior. The impact includes unauthorized application execution, persistence, and evasion of detection mechanisms. Mitigation strategies involve monitoring plist file changes, employing file integrity monitoring, and restricting plist file modifications to authorized users only.
